facts_disclosed: Switched to a Vauxhall Vivaro — got a good deal (replacing Ford Transit Custom)

turns.content: right so big update on the work front. finally pulled the trigger and got a new van
Nice one! That's a big move. What did you go for?
vauxhall vivaro. got a wicked deal on it through a bloke that Simran knows from the school. hes got a mate who runs a dealership in Solihull

The Vivaro is a solid choice -- they're well regarded for trade use. Good payload, reliable, and the running costs tend to be reasonable. Getting a deal through a contact is the best way to buy a van too, you avoid all the dealer markup rubbish. What spec did you go for? Panel van or crew cab?

panel van long wheelbase. I needed the space cos the old ford was doing my head in. every time I loaded up for a big job the pipe runs wouldnt fit without leaving the back doors half open. this one is sorted, loads more room in the back

Long wheelbase is the way to go for plumbing work, especially with full pipe runs. Not having to leave the doors cracked open is a safety thing as much as anything -- stuff shifting around while you're driving is an accident waiting to happen. How's the cab feel compared to what you had before? Comfortable for those long days driving between jobs?

yeah its proper comfy actually. better seats than the ford had even when the ford was new. and the fuel economy is way better. the ford was drinking diesel like it was going out of fashion especially the last year or so

Better fuel economy makes a huge difference when you're doing trade miles every day -- that saving adds up fast over a year. If the Ford was getting thirsty on top of everything else, the running cost difference alone probably justifies the switch. Was the Ford giving you mechanical trouble too or was it mainly the fuel and the space?

mate the ford was falling apart. the last 6 months it was in the garage every other week. alternator went, then the clutch was slipping, then something with the EGR valve. I was spending more on repairs than the van was worth. at some point you just gotta accept its done innit

Absolutely -- there's a crossover point where repair costs exceed what the vehicle is worth and every pound you put in is just delaying the inevitable. Sounds like the Ford was well past that point. An alternator, clutch, and EGR valve in six months is basically the van telling you it's finished.

Did you trade it in or sell it separately?

traded it in yeah. didnt get much for it obviously but it was something off the price. the deal on the vivaro was already good so I wasnt complaining. all in I reckon we saved about 2 grand compared to what I was seeing online for the same spec

Saving two grand on a van purchase is brilliant -- that's real money, especially when you factor in that you'll also be saving on fuel and repairs going forward. Sounds like Simran's contact came through properly.

Have you got the racking and shelving sorted in the back yet, or is that the next job?

thats this weekends project. well not this weekend cos thats family time but next week in the evenings. I'm planning it all out properly this time. the ford was a mess by the end, tools everywhere, couldnt find anything. gonna do proper shelving with labelled boxes and everything

Starting fresh with a proper organisation system is one of the best parts of getting a new van. Labelled boxes are a game changer -- you'll save so much time on jobs not having to dig through piles of fittings to find the right one. Are you going to buy pre-made racking or build something custom?

bit of both probably. theres some decent modular racking on Screwfix that fits the vivaro and then I'll add some custom bits for the bigger tools. pipe vice needs its own spot, same with the press tool. the important thing is everything has a place

That's the right approach -- modular for the standard stuff and custom mounts for the specialist tools. The pipe vice and press tool definitely need dedicated spots, they're too heavy and awkward to be loose. If you measure everything up before you start, you can plan the layout so the tools you grab most often are the easiest to reach.

Sounds like the Vivaro is going to be a proper upgrade all round. Enjoy setting it up -- there's something satisfying about a freshly organised work van.
